premise: Kael Rusk, an under-ranked contract blade in the Rillgate Contract Hall, first confronts the debt pressure that will sell his sister's marker, then accepts a cheap Gray Salt Mine retrieval job in Chapter 2 to earn bronze rank. The mine is really a sealed ruin where a rival crew and a hidden patron are harvesting outlaw monster cores, but that operation is first discovered in Chapter 6.

characters.name: Kael Rusk
characters.description: Under-ranked contract blade, late 20s. Uses iron-thread binding, a dangerous technique that turns monster pressure into short-lived weapon patterns at a bodily cost. Needs bronze rank to keep his sister Mira's debt marker inside Rillgate protection. Tactical, proud, and too quick to take the cleanest payout before anyone can use him.

characters.name: Tessa Mire
characters.description: Rival spearhand from a ranked crew. Her sponsor pushes her into the Gray Salt Mine, making her both competition and the witness Kael needs if he wants his evidence to survive guild law.

characters.name: Orin Vale
characters.description: Rillgate contract clerk who knows salvage clauses, witness rules, and rank-law loopholes. He can turn Kael's proof into legal standing only if Kael preserves admissible evidence.

characters.name: Lady Varn
characters.description: Polite contract broker and hidden ruin-rights patron. She prices the mine job low so unranked crews trigger illegal brine wards and leave ranked buyers unblamed.

lockedCharacters.name: Kael Rusk
lockedCharacters.mustHaveArc: Moves from grabbing the clean payout alone to using witness testimony and contract law as leverage.

lockedCharacters.name: Tessa Mire
lockedCharacters.mustHaveArc: Shifts from rival claimant to necessary witness whose testimony changes Kael's legal standing.

lockedCharacters.name: Orin Vale
lockedCharacters.mustHaveArc: Turns from warning Kael off the job to filing the evidence that grants provisional bronze rank.

lockedCharacters.name: Lady Varn
mustHaveTraits: uses cheap contracts as disposable triggers
lockedCharacters.mustHaveArc: Stays partially protected in Book 1 and becomes the pressure behind the next restricted contract.

requiredBeats.description: MPA-01 Hub pressure: Kael learns Mira's debt marker will be sold unless he gains bronze rank or equivalent leverage before the next contract bell. Fill the chapter with 8-10 hub/debt-pressure scenes: contract hall search, Orin's warning, debt market proof, creditor pressure, failed safe-job options, rank-law constraint, Mira stakes, and Lady Varn as only the next lead.
mustInclude: Orin Vale warns Kael that law, not courage, decides rank protection
Lady Varn is only a lead for Chapter 2
Kael has not met Lady Varn yet
Kael has not accepted the Gray Salt Mine contract yet
Chapter 1 endpoint leaves the contract unsigned

requiredBeats.description: MPA-02 Contract offer: Kael first meets Lady Varn, evaluates her cheap Gray Salt Mine retrieval job, signs it, checks the legal weakness with Orin, prepares under Mira's debt deadline, and leaves Rillgate. Fill the chapter with 8-10 contract-launch scenes, not a compressed summary.
mustInclude: Mira debt deadline pressure before departure
Kael accepts and leaves for the job only in this chapter

requiredBeats.description: MPA-03 Crew friction: Tessa's ranked crew claim collides with Kael's unranked bid outside the Gray Salt Mine, forcing a witness/salvage problem and joint-entry plan before any tunnel descent.
mustInclude: ally or rival pressure changes the job plan
chapter endpoint remains outside the mine
the illegal core harvest is not revealed yet

requiredBeats.description: MPA-04 Arena entry: The flooded Gray Salt Mine immediately constrains tactics with brine wards, rank-token responses, and unsafe salvage routes.
mustInclude: first real brine-ward danger happens here

requiredBeats.description: MPA-05 First tactical win: Kael uses iron-thread binding at a named bodily cost to win a route, ward pattern, brine-hook hint, or survival resource. This is a false tactical gain before the midpoint reveal, not the hidden-operation reveal.
mustInclude: false tactical win before midpoint reveal
sealed door or route pressure only
the sealed chamber, monster core, and illegal harvest remain unrevealed

requiredBeats.description: MPA-06 Job complication and midpoint reveal: Kael and Tessa first reach the sealed ruin chamber and discover the retrieval target is part of an illegal ruin-rights harvest, not the harmless mine recovery described on the contract.
mustInclude: mission target is not what the contract claimed

requiredBeats.description: MPA-07 Progression trial: Kael must refine iron-thread binding under pressure, choosing between a stronger technique and a worse lingering injury.
MPA-08 Faction reveal: Evidence links Lady Varn's brokers to a larger ruin-rights buyer, making the job a faction problem rather than a mine accident.
mustInclude: larger conflict exposed but not resolved

requiredBeats.description: MPA-09 Contract climax: Kael completes, subverts, or renegotiates the objective by saving Tessa as a witness instead of taking the clean core payout.
mustInclude: evidence survives because Kael refuses the clean payout

requiredBeats.description: MPA-10 Return and next hook: Kael returns to Rillgate with contested proof, gains provisional bronze status, protects Mira, and receives a restricted next contract tied to Varn's buyers.
mustInclude: status changes and next mission pressure appears

forbidden: Do not make Kael a chosen-one heir.
Do not add stat sheets or explicit LitRPG interfaces.
Do not position Book 1 as harem, academy, or base-building.
Do not resolve the entire ruin-rights faction problem in Book 1.
Do not let worldbuilding remain decorative; guild law, mine rules, or monster ecology must constrain choices.
Do not have Kael sign or leave for the Gray Salt Mine in Chapter 1; Chapter 1 is hub/debt pressure only.
Do not repeat contract evaluation, signing, or departure after Chapter 2.
Do not reveal the sealed ruin chamber, monster-core harvest, or Lady Varn's illegal operation before Chapter 6.
Do not repeat first brine-ward discovery after Chapter 4; later mine chapters must escalate from known arena rules.

structuralConstraints.povRotation: Single POV through Kael Rusk unless the planner has a concrete reason to use a brief alternate witness perspective.
structuralConstraints.pacing: One contained paid mission: hub pressure, contract, crew friction, arena, tactical win, complication, progression trial, faction reveal, climax, return hook.

storyThreads.label: Rillgate contract loop
storyThreads.description: Paid contracts, rank law, salvage rights, and witness rules decide who gets paid and who gains standing.

storyThreads.label: Iron-thread progression
storyThreads.description: Kael's technique improves only when he pays a named bodily cost under monster pressure.

storyThreads.label: Ruin-rights patronage
storyThreads.description: Hidden patrons launder illegal ruin harvests through cheap contracts and disposable crews.

storyDebts.promiseText: Kael must earn bronze rank or equivalent legal leverage before Mira's debt marker leaves guild protection.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off through provisional bronze rank or guild leverage created by admissible contract evidence.

storyDebts.promiseText: The cheap Gray Salt Mine job must reveal why the danger, patron, or objective was hidden.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off when Kael proves the retrieval job was a rigged ruin-rights harvest.

storyDebts.promiseText: Kael and Tessa must shift from rival claimants to necessary witnesses.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off when Tessa's testimony changes Kael's legal standing and future options.

storyDebts.promiseText: The mine job must point to a stronger patron or next contract rather than resolving the whole faction problem.
storyDebts.payoffPolicy: Pay off with a restricted next mission tied to Lady Varn's buyers.

storyPayoffs.payoffText: Kael returns with admissible evidence and gains provisional bronze standing before Mira's marker is sold.
The climax proves the mine retrieval was priced to trigger an illegal ruin-rights harvest.
Tessa's testimony makes Kael's claim legal instead of merely heroic.
Orin offers Kael a restricted next job tied to Lady Varn's hidden buyers.

chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael searches the Contract Hall, learns Mira's marker deadline, tests safe options, and leaves with Lady Varn only as the next lead.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Kael understands he needs a contract and admissible evidence before the debt bell, but he has not met Lady Varn and has not signed.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Kael goes to the first Lady Varn meeting in Chapter 2.

chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael first meets Lady Varn, evaluates the cheap Gray Salt Mine job, signs, checks legal weakness with Orin, feels Mira's debt deadline tighten, equips, and leaves Rillgate.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Kael has accepted the job and leaves with the witness/salvage weakness still unresolved.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Chapter 3 begins at the mine approach with Tessa's rival crew already present.
prohibitedMovement: Mine entrance rivalry as completed scene

chapterContracts.storyFunction: Rival claimant and witness/salvage problem before arena entry.
chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael reaches the mine approach, clashes with Tessa, compares claims, negotiates witness terms, and sets the joint-entry plan outside the mine.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Tessa agrees to witness terms and the crews prepare outside; the actual mine-entry hazard has not started.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Chapter 4 owns first descent, first brine-ward activation, and first arena-rule danger.

chapterContracts.storyFunction: First arena entry and brine-ward danger.
chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael and Tessa enter the flooded mine, trigger or narrowly trigger brine wards, and learn the arena rules under pressure.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Kael understands the ward rules well enough to survive the immediate hazard, while the hidden operation remains unrevealed.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Chapter 5 owns the first costly tactical win using iron-thread binding.

chapterContracts.storyFunction: Costly route/ward-pattern win before midpoint reveal.
chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael uses iron-thread binding at visible bodily cost to win a route, ward-pattern read, brine-hook hint, or survival resource.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Kael has a tactical advantage or route clue, but he has not opened the sealed chamber or understood the hidden operation.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Chapter 6 owns the midpoint reveal inside the sealed chamber.

chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael and Tessa first enter the sealed chamber and discover the retrieval target is part of an illegal monster-core harvest.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: The harmless retrieval job is revealed as a rigged illegal harvest, changing the mission from retrieval to evidence survival.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Chapter 7 tests Kael's progression under the revealed threat.

chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael refines iron-thread binding under live pressure and accepts a worse lingering injury to keep evidence and Tessa alive.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Kael gains a stronger technique at a lasting cost and can no longer solve the job alone.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Chapter 8 follows the faction trail without resolving it.

chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Evidence points beyond Lady Varn to buyers/patrons, deepening the faction problem while keeping the mission contained.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Kael and Tessa know the mine points to a larger network, but the book still has to preserve evidence and survive the contract climax.

chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael chooses evidence and witness survival over a clean payout, preserving the legal path at tactical cost.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Kael exits with enough evidence and witness support to make a legal claim, not just a heroic claim.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Chapter 10 owns return, provisional status, Mira protection, and next hook.

chapterContracts.storyFunction: Return-to-hub status change and next mission hook.
chapterContracts.ownedMovement: Kael and Tessa return to Rillgate, Orin files admissible evidence, Mira's marker is protected, and the next restricted mission appears.
chapterContracts.requiredEndpoint: Kael saves Mira through legal standing but enters a larger conflict with Lady Varn's buyers.
chapterContracts.handoffToNext: Book 2 begins from the restricted mission.

chapterSequenceGuards.description: Chapter 1 owns hub/debt pressure only; Kael may see the Gray Salt Mine option but must not accept, sign, depart, or arrive.
mustNotContain: offers him a cup of tea
asks pointed questions about salvage rights

chapterSequenceGuards.description: Chapter 2 owns the contract decision and departure.
Chapter 2 should leave the hub and road open for Chapter 3's mine approach, not consume the rival-claimant arrival.

Chapter 3 owns rival claimant and witness/salvage friction at the mine approach or threshold; it must not enter the tunnel, trigger brine wards, create rank-token danger, or reveal the illegal harvest.
Chapter 4 owns first real brine-ward danger and arena-rule discovery, but not the illegal core-harvest reveal.
Chapter 5 owns a costly tactical win and partial gain; the illegal harvest reveal belongs to Chapter 6.
Chapter 6 is the first reveal that the Gray Salt Mine job is an illegal ruin-rights/core-harvest operation.

directives.rawNotes:
Method pack: mercenary-progression-adventure-v0. Book 1 contract packet: series promise = one paid Rillgate mission changes Kael's rank, technique, ally network, and next-job pressure; protagonist progression axis = iron-thread binding gains a brine-hook pattern only through a lingering bodily cost; guild/hub rule = rank, witness, and salvage clauses decide whether action becomes legal standing; Book 1 contract = retrieve a missing survey chain and salt-sealed core from the Gray Salt Mine for low pay; arena = flooded mine over sealed ruin with brine wards responding to blood, rank tokens, and stolen cores; hidden complication = Lady Varn priced the job to make unranked crews trigger illegal wards for a patron; party shift = Tessa moves from rival claimant to necessary witness; faction consequence = Rillgate learns a polite broker is laundering ruin-rights theft through cheap contracts; next hook = Kael receives a restricted next mission aimed at Varn's buyers.
Chapter separation guard: Ch1 is hub/debt pressure only and must not include the Lady Varn office scene, the contract offer, signing, or departure. Ch1 should still be a full 8-10 scene chapter using only hub/debt pressure: contract hall search, Orin's warning, debt market proof, creditor pressure, failed safe-job options, rank-law constraint, Mira stakes, and Lady Varn as only the next lead. Ch1 may end with Orin naming Lady Varn as a lead, a warning, a debt-market deadline, or Lady Varn's address as the next stop, but no broker meeting, no office scene, no contract terms, no witness desk, no signature, no contract copy, and no road departure. Ch2 owns the first Lady Varn meeting, contract evaluation, signing, preparation, and departure only; do not repeat this movement later. Ch3 is rival claimant and salvage/witness friction at the mine approach or threshold only; use outside story work such as road arrival, Tessa's camp, contract comparison, sponsor-pressure suspicion, witness-term negotiation, equipment limits, night watch, and a joint entry plan. Ch3 endpoint: Tessa agrees to witness terms and both crews prepare outside; the chapter ends before any tunnel descent, flooded chamber, brine-ward activation, or rank-token danger. Ch4 is first mine entry, first real brine-ward activation, and first pressure hazard; do not repeat first discovery later. Ch5 is first costly tactical win and false tactical gain before the midpoint reveal: Kael wins a route, ward-pattern read, brine-hook hint, or survival resource only. Ch5 may reach a sealed door or route clue but must not open the sealed chamber, identify a monster core, or understand that the job is an illegal harvest. Ch6 is the midpoint reveal and first sealed-chamber/core-harvest reveal. Ch7 is the progression trial. Ch8 is the faction reveal. Ch9 is the contract climax. Ch10 is return/status/next hook.
First two chapter scene pressure notes:
Ch1 scene A objectivePressure=Kael must get a bronze path before Mira's marker sale; tacticalConstraint=rank law blocks direct protection; allyMateriality=Orin explains the clause that makes evidence more useful than bravado; endpoint=Kael needs a contract, not a fight.
Ch1 scene B objectivePressure=Kael tries to buy time with a creditor; tacticalConstraint=the marker can be sold outside guild jurisdiction; worldFactPressure=Rillgate protection depends on active rank; endpoint=Kael leaves with a lead and a deadline, but the contract remains unsigned and the first Lady Varn meeting is deferred to Ch2.
Ch2 scene A objectivePressure=Kael first meets Lady Varn and evaluates her salt-mine job; tacticalConstraint=low pay signals hidden danger but no ranked crew wants it; factionPressure=Varn's civil terms make the trap legal on paper; endpoint=Kael accepts because the job is the only rank path.
Ch2 scene B objectivePressure=Kael secures tools and clauses before departure; tacticalConstraint=salvage rights require a witness or bronze token; allyMateriality=Orin warns that admissible proof matters more than the retrieved object; endpoint=the job leaves the hall with a legal weakness Kael must solve.
